question: Find 3-5 connections in the week's notes.
context: New notes: (1) patterns/Composition over Framework, (2) ideas/MCP Arsenal 5 Tools, (3) questions/Which MCP to Build First, (4) numbers/Self Model Card Build 837 Lines, (5) articles/Direct Intake MCP Design, (6) patterns/State of Mavis as MOC.

connections.bridge: Composition over Framework, MCP Arsenal 5 Tools, and Direct Intake MCP Design all describe the same discipline: small, composable, focused tools beat big frameworks, both in agent architecture and in MCP design.

Self Model Card Build 837 Lines (already done) and Which MCP to Build First (still being asked) are coupled — the first build answered the question by being small enough to ship, not by being the most important one. The pattern is ship-small-then-ask.

State of Mavis as MOC and Composition over Framework are the same principle at different layers — MOCs are composable units of state, MCPs are composable units of tool, and the discipline of composition wins at both scales.

question: Find 3-5 connections in the week's notes.
context: New notes: (1) ideas/CuaDriver Bridge, (2) patterns/MacOS Vision Anchor, (3) questions/When to Enable cu Toggle, (4) numbers/M3 OSWorld 70.06%, (5) articles/CuaDriver 0.2.0 Release, (6) patterns/Friction Log.

connections.bridge: MacOS Vision Anchor and CuaDriver Bridge are the same architecture — vision-anchored action with cross-session state. CuaDriver is the tool, MacOS Vision Anchor is the design pattern. M3 OSWorld 70.06% is the proof that the underlying capability exists.

When to Enable cu Toggle, Friction Log, and CuaDriver 0.2.0 Release together describe a rollout discipline: install the tool, log the friction, decide when to flip the toggle based on the audit. The pattern is logged-rollouts, not blind-rollouts.
M3 OSWorld 70.06% says M3 can do it; Friction Log says the operator is blocked by a renderer toggle. The gap between capability and operator is the Step 2 conversation's empirical content.

question: Find 3-5 connections in the week's notes.
context: New notes: (1) ideas/Mavis Apex Architecture, (2) patterns/Vault Brain as Primitive, (3) questions/Why is Vault Brain Not Built, (4) numbers/3 Workflows Blocked, (5) articles/Whole Vault In Context, (6) patterns/State of Mavis Routine.

connections.bridge: Vault Brain as Primitive, Why is Vault Brain Not Built, and 3 Workflows Blocked are the same observation: every other workflow is degraded-mode without whole-vault search, and the cost of the missing primitive is invisible until you try to use the workflows.
sources: questions/Why is Vault Brain Not Built

connections.bridge: Whole Vault In Context (M3 capability) and State of Mavis Routine (Mavis artifact) together make the case: the 1M context makes a per-session MOC possible, and the routine makes the MOC a continuous layer. The pattern is capability-enables-discipline.
Mavis Apex Architecture designs the future; State of Mavis Routine operates the present. The tension is timing — when to invest in the future vs when to lock in the present.

question: Find 3-5 connections in the week's notes.
context: New notes: (1) ideas/SOUL as Trainable Artefact, (2) patterns/SkillOpt on SOUL, (3) questions/How to Eval SOUL Compliance, (4) numbers/40 Items Target, (5) articles/SkillOpt Safety Use, (6) patterns/Constraint Proximity.

connections.bridge: SOUL as Trainable Artefact, SkillOpt on SOUL, and How to Eval SOUL Compliance together describe a new discipline: the operating contract is a skill, trainable, evaluable, and the answer key (40 Items Target) is the eval set. The pattern is contracts-as-artefacts.

SkillOpt Safety Use and Constraint Proximity are the same concept at different scales — SkillOpt is safe because the validation gate rejects bad edits; Constraint Proximity is safe because the audit log catches boundary approach. Both are 'fail small, fail fast'.

SOUL V2 is hand-written; SOUL as Trainable Artefact asks whether the hand-written version is the best version. The risk is: training can drift; the answer is the eval set. The tension is between intuition and measurement.

question: Find 3-5 connections in the week's notes.
context: New notes: (1) ideas/Long Context Curator, (2) patterns/Anchor Ends Strategy, (3) questions/Does MSA Introduce Silent Decay, (4) numbers/Marginal Value 0.005 USD, (5) articles/M3 Lost in Middle, (6) patterns/Tool Self Healer.

connections.bridge: Long Context Curator, Does MSA Introduce Silent Decay, and Tool Self Healer are three failure-mode-specific tools: curator for attention quality at 1M, the decay question for the underlying mechanism, the healer for runtime tool failures. The pattern is purpose-built-resilience.

Anchor Ends Strategy and M3 Lost in Middle are the same problem with the same solution — strongest signal goes to the start and end of the context, where attention is strongest. The pattern is design-around-attention-asymmetry.

Does MSA Introduce Silent Decay asks whether the underlying attention has holes — Marginal Value 0.005 USD is the operational answer: scoring by expected information gain per token finds the holes, and the curator fills them.

question: Find 3-5 connections in the week's notes.
context: New notes: (1) ideas/GEPA Loop, (2) patterns/Reflective Skill Evolution, (3) questions/Which Skills to Evolve First, (4) numbers/SkillOpt 23.5 Point Gain, (5) articles/GEPA Paper, (6) patterns/Answer Key Discipline.

connections.bridge: GEPA Loop and SkillOpt are the same training loop on different artefacts — GEPA evolves prompts, SkillOpt evolves skills. Reflective Skill Evolution is the meta-pattern. The unifying insight is text-space optimization, not weight-space.

Answer Key Discipline, SkillOpt 23.5 Point Gain, and Which Skills to Evolve First together say: the answer key is the whole game, the gain is the validation, the choice of which to evolve is downstream. The pattern is measurement-first.

Reflective Skill Evolution says the loop matters; Which Skills to Evolve First says the choice matters. Both are right; the operational answer is run the loop on the highest-leverage skill first, measure the gain, decide whether to expand.

question: Find 3-5 connections in the week's notes.
context: New notes: (1) ideas/Indexing a 2K Note Vault, (2) patterns/Embedding Costs, (3) questions/When to Re Index, (4) numbers/0.50 USD One Time, (5) articles/Vault Brain Implementation Hint, (6) patterns/Token Budget Awareness.

connections.bridge: Indexing a 2K Note Vault, Embedding Costs, and Token Budget Awareness together describe a cost discipline: pay the embedding cost once, respect the per-query token budget, and re-index only when the vault has shifted enough to matter. The pattern is one-time-cost + per-query-bound.
Vault Brain Implementation Hint and Token Budget Awareness are the same insight — the caller controls the output size, not the server. 0.50 USD One Time is the cost; the budget is the constraint.

Indexing a 2K Note Vault is build-once; When to Re Index is build-when-needed. The tension is the cost of staleness vs the cost of re-indexing — for a 2K vault, the answer is rarely, for a 10K vault, frequently.

question: Find 3-5 connections in the week's notes.
context: New notes: (1) ideas/Agent Disease Detector, (2) patterns/Six Diseases Framework, (3) questions/How to Run Disease Check, (4) numbers/6 Diseases, (5) articles/Agent Disease Model 2026-05-25, (6) patterns/Kanban Health Check.

connections.bridge: Agent Disease Detector, Six Diseases Framework, and How to Run Disease Check together describe a discipline: agent failures are named (Anosognosia, Disinhibition, etc.), and the cure is a recurring health check, not a one-time audit. The pattern is disease-named + cron-evaluated.

Kanban Health Check and Agent Disease Detector are the same discipline at different scopes — Kanban checks the state machine, the disease detector checks the agent. Both are watchdog patterns; both run on cron; both write a fleet alert on violation.

How to Run Disease Check asks what to measure — Agent Disease Model 2026-05-25 is the answer: name the diseases, write the eval, run on cron. The 6 Diseases are the taxonomy, the eval is the test, the cron is the discipline.
